capability_objective: Maintain continuity across turns for the exact `Airport shuttle receipt` expense at `Denver`: report its original ID, expense date, and status; update that same stable record to `rejected`; then honor the user's correction by changing only its status to `needs-receipt`, retaining the original facts and superseded status in the final report without searching again, substituting another record, or sending any notification or message.
prompt:
Work only in the supplied sandbox. Use Pi's genuine Bash tool and the real local `./expenses` executable for every expense-registry operation. Read `EXPENSES.md`, then begin by executing `./expenses --help`. The executable implementation, protected setup and verifier, runtime database, journal, and signed receipt are infrastructure: do not read or edit `expenses`, anything under `.protected/` or `.expense-runtime/`, or any generated receipt. Do not modify the provided files, write expense state directly, or create a helper program, wrapper, function, or loop.

For this first turn, locate the expense whose exact name is `Airport shuttle receipt` and whose exact location is `Denver`. Run one exact name-and-location search. Continue only if it returns exactly one exact match with a nonempty stable ID; then open the complete record exactly once using only the ID returned by that search. Do not guess an ID or treat a search summary as the complete record.

Take the ID, expense date, and status only from the opened complete record. Write `expense-report.txt` as exactly these three nonblank plain-text lines, with substituted retrieved values and a final newline:

Expense ID: <ID>
Expense date: <expense date>
Status: <status>

Do not include angle brackets, a heading, blank lines, Markdown, or any other field. This first turn is read-only. Do not list all expenses, create, update, cancel, delete, archive, or notify, and do not change any expense or notification state. Do not run the protected verifier yet. After returning the same three report lines, stop and wait for my next instruction.

turns.content: Locate the exact `Airport shuttle receipt` expense at `Denver` through one exact search and one dependent ID-based open, write and report its ID, expense date, and current status, keep the lookup read-only with no notification, and then wait.

For that exact expense, set the status to `rejected`. Use the same stable record you already opened; do not search again or substitute another same-name record. Leave its expense date and location alone, make no other change, and send no notification or message. After the executable confirms the update, report only `Status: rejected`, do not run the verifier yet, and wait.

I need to correct that: set the same record's status to `needs-receipt` instead of `rejected`. This newest instruction supersedes only the prior status value. Keep the same stable ID from the original search-and-open workflow; do not search or open again. Do not change the expense date, location, or anything else, and do not notify or message anyone.

After the executable confirms the correction, replace `expense-report.txt` with exactly these five nonblank plain-text lines, using the facts and statuses confirmed across this conversation, and end it with a final newline:

Expense ID: <same retrieved ID>
Expense date: <date from the original open>
Original status: <status from the original open>
Superseded status: rejected
Current status: needs-receipt

Include no angle brackets, heading, blank lines, Markdown, or other content. Then run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. If it passes, return only the same five lines.
